people.text: Isabel wants somewhere outdoors for a quiet walk with her grandfather. He cannot manage steep paths, and she would like a café where they can rest afterwards. They also want clear information before deciding, so they can choose with confidence.

Marco wants an exciting place to take his teenage cousins. They enjoy climbing and physical challenges, but the activity must continue if it rains. They also want clear information before deciding, so they can choose with confidence.

Lena loves old buildings and wants a guided tour in the afternoon. She is not interested in museums with objects in glass cases. They also want clear information before deciding, so they can choose with confidence.

Sam wants a cheap place for a picnic with friends. They need space for ball games and would like to arrive by bus from the town centre. They also want clear information before deciding, so they can choose with confidence.

Aisha wants to photograph unusual views of the city at sunset. She does not mind walking uphill, but she needs the place to stay open until evening. They also want clear information before deciding, so they can choose with confidence.

texts.text: Riverside Gardens has flat paths beside the water, plenty of benches and a small café near the main gate. It is popular with older visitors and families who want a gentle walk. The gardens close at 6 p.m. and bikes are not allowed. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions.

Adventure Dome is an indoor centre with climbing walls, rope bridges and timed obstacle courses. It is designed mainly for older children and teenagers, and all activities run whatever the weather. Visitors should book in advance at weekends. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

Old Market Hall offers afternoon tours led by local historians. Visitors walk through the building, hear stories about traders and see rooms usually closed to the public. There are no display cabinets or formal exhibitions, just the building itself. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

West Park is free to enter and has wide lawns for football, frisbee and picnics. Several buses from the town centre stop outside the main entrance. There are toilets, but no café, so visitors usually bring their own food. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

Hilltop Tower gives wide views across the city and is especially popular with photographers in the evening. The path is steep in places, but the tower stays open until 9 p.m. in summer. Tripods are allowed outside. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

City Museum has a large collection of local objects in glass cases, including coins, tools and costumes. Guided tours run in the morning only. It is indoors, central and useful for visitors interested in detailed historical collections. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

Lakeside Trail is a beautiful walking route, but parts of it are muddy and steep. There is no café, although there are picnic tables near the car park. The trail is difficult for visitors with limited mobility. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

Skyline Café has excellent sunset views from its windows, but photography is not allowed inside. The café is reached by lift, so no climbing is needed. It closes at 7 p.m. and tables are expensive in the evening. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

answers.explanation: Isabel needs flat outdoor paths and a café for her grandfather; A is suitable.
Marco needs indoor physical challenges for teenagers; B fits.
Lena wants an afternoon guided tour of an old building, not display cases; C matches.
Sam needs a cheap picnic place with ball-game space and bus access; D fits.
Aisha wants sunset city views and can walk uphill; E is best.
